description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden roof supported by two sturdy wooden posts. The roof is constructed from overlapping wooden planks, forming a peaked structure with visible metal fasteners. A wooden crank handle and spindle are mounted horizontally between the posts, with a rope wound around the spindle and a wooden bucket attached for drawing water. The stone base is polygonal and robust, with a metallic spout or decorative element on one side. The well appears weathered, with wood grain and stone textures clearly visible, and the bucket rests on the edge of the well opening.
description: A classic stone water well with a peaked wooden roof, crank handle, and bucket, featuring detailed wood and stone textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the peaked wooden roof, crank handle, and bucket resting on the stone well's edge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden crank mechanism, bucket, and the open well mouth with visible rope.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the wooden roof and the stone base, with less visibility of the crank and bucket.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the crank handle, open well mouth, and the bucket positioned on the edge.
attribute_well_cover_design: Peaked wooden roof made of planks, no full cover over the well mouth; open access for bucket.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible wooden crank handle and spindle with rope and wooden bucket attached, mounted between posts.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, forming the well's main protective barrier.
